page: Microsoft Azure D192ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 96 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores, operating at a base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor architecture is x86_64, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 384.0 GB of memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-threaded compute and memory bandwidth workloads. The server achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 197908.53 and a PassMark CPU score of 150927.69, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory read bandwidth reaches 14747.07 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 685.05 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2252.84, placing it in the middle 50%. Large-model LLM inference shows a split profile, achieving top-tier prompt processing at 40.71 tokens/sec but dropping to an average 4.67 tokens/sec for text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D192ls_v7 offers high compute density and massive parallel processing capabilities relative to its memory footprint. This makes it highly efficient for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive RAM arrays. It is well-suited for high-throughput workloads such as multi-threaded decompression, database operations, static web serving, and prompt processing for large language models. The lack of local storage requires external volume attachments for persistent data.
description: Microsoft Azure D192ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, powered by a 192-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 384.0 GB of memory, representing a low-memory configuration of 2.0 GB per vCPU, with no local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 150927.69 and exceptional memory read bandwidth of up to 14747.07 GB/sec. While single-core performance is average, its multi-threaded capabilities excel, as demonstrated by a bzip3 decompression speed of 952.09 GB/sec and 265.88 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. This server is highly efficient for parallelized, CPU-heavy workloads such as database operations, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D192ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 192 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for high-throughput workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D192ls_v7 is a 192-vCPU compute-optimized server with 384 GB RAM, offering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs and exceptional multi-threaded performance for CPU-intensive, low-memory workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6973P-C processors at 4.2 GHz.
384.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Top-tier multi-core performance with a PassMark CPU score of 150927.69.
High-speed memory bandwidth reaching up to 14747.07 GB/sec.
Optimized for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression.
No local storage or GPUs, requiring external storage attachments.
